fingerprint: The dome's 'disc_thickness' parameter (12,717 km) is Earth's diameter (12,742 km) relabeled. The formula f=c/(2D)=11.787 Hz only works with this globe-derived value — no dome structural parameter produces this frequency. Additionally, Tesla's velocity 471,240 km/s ≈ πc/2, the phase velocity of the spherical Earth-ionosphere waveguide.

dome_values.name: disc_thickness
dome_values.note: No dome parameter equals this. Disc radius=20,015 km, firmament=9,086 km, disc diameter=40,030 km. The value 12,717 km is ONLY Earth's diameter.

dome_values.name: Tesla Longitudinal Constant (va)
dome_values.note: Matches πc/2 = 471,239 km/s to 0.09%. This is the ELF phase velocity of a spherical shell waveguide.

why_it_matters: This is a triple-layered globe fingerprint: (1) The frequency 11.78 Hz can only be obtained from Earth's diameter, not any dome dimension. (2) The velocity constant πc/2 is a structural property of spherical shell waveguides — it arises from the ratio of Earth's circumference to its diameter, which is π by definition for a sphere. On a flat disc, there is no geometric reason for this ratio to equal π/2. (3) The dome's formula f=c/(2D) and Tesla's formulation v=πc/2 traveling over circumference are algebraically identical BECAUSE circumference = π × diameter — a relationship that holds for spheres. The dome obtains the correct answer only because it unknowingly encodes the globe's geometry.
algebraic_proof: Tesla: t = 2 × circumference / v = 2πR_earth / (πc/2) = 4R_earth/c. So f = 1/t = c/(4R) = c/(2D). The formula f=c/(2D) is not an independent disc-resonance equation — it is Tesla's spherical surface-wave calculation with the sphere's geometry collapsed into a single variable D. The π cancels because both the path length (circumference = 2πR) and the velocity (πc/2) contain π. The dome presents the result after the cancellation, hiding the spherical origin.
suggested_text: The dome's 'disc_thickness' of 12,717 km matches Earth's diameter (12,742 km) to 0.2% — but corresponds to no dome structural parameter. More revealing still: Tesla's velocity of 471,240 km/s equals πc/2, the phase velocity of ELF waves in a spherical shell waveguide. The formula f = c/(2D) is simply Tesla's spherical calculation with π cancelled out: the surface path (2πR) at speed πc/2 gives round-trip time 4R/c, whose reciprocal is c/(2×2R) = c/(2D). Every element of this 'dome prediction' — the frequency, the velocity constant, and the formula itself — is the globe's geometry in disguise.
